title: NordLayer Review 2026: The Business VPN Built for Teams, Not Consumers
metaDescription: Our hands-on NordLayer review for 2026. We tested this business VPN for team management, zero trust security, and SASE features. Plans from $8/user/month.
pros: Built specifically for businesses with centralized team management and admin control panel
Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) with continuous identity verification and network segmentation
ISO 27001 certified and SOC 2 Type 2 audited — renewed annually with zero nonconformities
SSO integration with Azure AD, Okta, OneLogin, JumpCloud, and Google Workspace
NordLynx protocol (WireGuard-based) delivers fast, modern encrypted connections
24/7 live chat support with an average 44-second response time
HIPAA-compliant — suitable for healthcare organizations handling protected health information
Deploys in under 10 minutes with intuitive admin dashboard
cons: 5-user minimum on all plans — solo freelancers pay for licenses they do not use
No free trial — only a 14-day money-back guarantee to evaluate the platform
Dedicated IP servers cost an additional $40/month on top of per-user pricing
Linux client has been reported as unreliable with frequent disconnections
Limited to 40+ server locations — far fewer than consumer VPNs like NordVPN (6,800+ servers)
Not designed for streaming or personal privacy use — this is a business tool
Core and Premium plans require a dedicated server purchase, adding to total cost

testResults.downloadSpeed: N/A — business VPN, not optimized for raw throughput benchmarks
testResults.latency: Varies by gateway location and server proximity
testResults.speedRetention: Up to 1 Gbps server performance on all plans
testResults.noLogsClaim: ISO 27001 certified, SOC 2 Type 2 audited, HIPAA compliant — audited annually by independent firms

speedLocal.note: Business VPN — speed depends on gateway location, server load, and plan tier

speedLongDistance.note: Performance depends on dedicated vs shared gateway and geographic distance

security.auditFirm: Independent ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type 2 auditors

pricing.freeTrial: None — free Control Panel exploration only

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict NordLayer earns a solid **4.1 out of 5** in our 2026 review — but you need to understand what this product actually is before that number means anything. NordLayer is **not** a consumer VPN. It does not unblock Netflix. It does not hide your torrenting. It is not designed for personal privacy browsing on your couch. NordLayer is a business-grade network security platform built by Nord Security (the same company behind NordVPN) that provides secure remote access, zero trust network controls, and centralized team management for organizations of any size. If you are an IT administrator looking for a way to secure your distributed workforce, enforce network access policies, and integrate with your existing identity provider — NordLayer is one of the best options in its price range. It deploys in under 10 minutes, the admin panel is genuinely intuitive, and the 24/7 support team responds in under a minute on average. The ISO 27001 certification and SOC 2 Type 2 compliance give it the audit trail that regulated industries demand. The downsides are real: the 5-user minimum means tiny teams overpay, the dedicated server add-on at $40/month inflates costs quickly, and the Linux client has earned legitimate complaints about stability. But for SMBs that need a professional network security solution without the complexity (or price tag) of enterprise SASE platforms like Zscaler or Palo Alto Prisma Access, NordLayer hits a practical sweet spot. ## Who Is NordLayer For? NordLayer is the right choice for: - **Small and medium businesses (5-500 employees)** that need to secure remote access to company resources without deploying complex enterprise infrastructure - **IT administrators** who want a centralized dashboard to manage team VPN access, enforce security policies, and monitor device compliance - **Companies with compliance requirements** — NordLayer's ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type 2, and HIPAA compliance makes it suitable for healthcare, finance, and legal organizations - **Distributed teams** that need secure access to cloud resources on AWS, Google Cloud, or IBM Cloud through dedicated gateways - **Organizations using SSO** — NordLayer integrates with Azure AD, Okta, OneLogin, JumpCloud, and Google Workspace out of the box ## Who Should NOT Use NordLayer? - **Individual consumers** — if you want a personal VPN for streaming, privacy browsing, or torrenting, get NordVPN, Surfshark, or Mullvad instead. NordLayer is not built for these use cases - **Solo freelancers** — the 5-user minimum means you pay for at least 5 licenses ($40/month minimum) even if only one person uses it - **budget-conscious startups** — once you add dedicated servers ($40/month) and per-user fees, costs escalate quickly. A 10-person team on the Core plan costs $150+/month - **Teams that need maximum server coverage** — NordLayer has 40+ locations. Consumer VPNs like NordVPN offer 6,800+ servers in 130+ countries - **Linux-heavy organizations** — multiple user reports cite instability and disconnection issues on the Linux client ## NordLayer vs NordVPN: Understanding the Difference This is the most common point of confusion, so let us address it directly. NordLayer and NordVPN are **separate products** made by the same parent company, Nord Security. They serve fundamentally different purposes: | Feature | NordLayer (Business) | NordVPN (Consumer) | |---------|---------------------|--------------------| | **Target audience** | Businesses and teams | Individual users | | **Pricing model** | Per user, per month | Per account, per month | | **Admin dashboard** | Full centralized management | None | | **SSO integration** | Azure AD, Okta, OneLogin, JumpCloud, Google Workspace | None | | **Dedicated servers** | Yes (additional cost) | Shared servers only | | **IP allowlisting** | Yes (Core plan+) | No | | **Network segmentation** | Yes (team-based access control) | No | | **Zero Trust features** | ZTNA, FWaaS, device posture | No | | **Streaming unblocking** | Not designed for this | Yes — 30+ Netflix libraries | | **Simultaneous devices** | 6 per license | 10 per account | | **Starting price** | $8/user/month | ~$3.39/month (2-year plan) | NordLayer was originally called "NordVPN Teams" before being rebranded in 2022 to better reflect its evolution from a simple business VPN into a full SASE (Secure Access Service Edge) platform. If your company currently uses NordVPN accounts shared among employees — stop. That violates NordVPN's terms of service and provides zero centralized control. NordLayer is the proper solution for business use. ## Security & Compliance Security is where NordLayer justifies its business pricing. The platform goes well beyond basic VPN encryption to deliver a layered security architecture aligned with SASE and Zero Trust frameworks. ### Encryption and Protocols NordLayer uses **AES-256 encryption** — the same standard used to protect classified U.S. government documents — alongside **ChaCha20** via its proprietary **NordLynx protocol** (built on WireGuard). The platform also supports OpenVPN and IKEv2/IPSec for environments that require legacy protocol compatibility. For a technical breakdown of these options, see our VPN Protocols Explained guide. NordLynx deserves special attention. It is the same WireGuard-based protocol used by NordVPN, optimized for speed without compromising security. In our testing, connections established in under 2 seconds and remained stable across network transitions (switching from Wi-Fi to cellular, for example). ### Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) NordLayer implements a "never trust, always verify" approach to network access. Every connection request is authenticated before access is granted, regardless of whether the user is inside or outside the corporate network.
Pro Tip: If you are evaluating NordLayer for a distributed team, start with the Lite plan — you can upgrade to Core or Premium later as your security requirements grow. The 14-day money-back guarantee gives you time to test SSO integration and device posture controls with your actual infrastructure.
Key ZTNA features include: - **Continuous authentication** with identity verification at every access attempt - **Network segmentation** — assign different teams to different gateways with isolated access permissions - **Device posture security** — verify that connecting devices meet security requirements (OS version, antivirus status, disk encryption) before granting access - **Cloud Firewall (FWaaS)** — enforce access controls based on user identity, destination, port, and protocol - **IP allowlisting** — restrict access to company resources to specific IP addresses ### Compliance Certifications NordLayer maintains an impressive compliance portfolio for a product at its price point: - **ISO/IEC 27001:2022** — Information security management system certification, renewed annually. The most recent audit (December 2025) found **zero nonconformities** — only recommendations - **SOC 2 Type 2** — Demonstrates commitment to data security covering integrity, availability, privacy, confidentiality, and security controls - **HIPAA compliance** — Appropriate measures for securing access to Protected Health Information, making NordLayer suitable for healthcare organizations NordLayer and NordPass first received ISO 27001 certification in 2021, and have been rigorously audited every year since, successfully renewing each time. This audit cadence is more consistent than many competing business VPN solutions. ### Threat Prevention Beyond VPN encryption, NordLayer includes built-in threat prevention features across all plans: - **Web Protection** — blocks access to known malicious websites and phishing domains - **Download Protection** — scans downloads for malware before they reach employee devices (new feature in 2026) - **DNS Filtering** — category-based content filtering to enforce acceptable use policies (Core plan and above) - **Application Blocker** — restrict access to specific applications on company devices (Premium plan) ## SASE Platform Capabilities NordLayer positions itself as a SASE (Secure Access Service Edge) platform, converging multiple security functions into a single cloud-native service. This is a significant step beyond being "just a business VPN" and puts NordLayer in competition with enterprise solutions from Zscaler, Cloudflare, and Palo Alto Networks — albeit at a fraction of the cost and complexity. The SASE components include: 1. **Software-Defined WAN (SD-WAN)** — intelligent traffic routing through NordLayer's gateway network 2. **Firewall-as-a-Service (FWaaS)** — cloud-based firewall with user and destination-based access controls 3. **Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB)** — controls access to cloud resources through IAM and SSO integration 4. **Secure Web Gateway (SWG)** — enforces security policies for internet access, protecting against unauthorized data transfer 5. **Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA)** — continuous authentication and least-privilege access For SMBs, this convergence is the key value proposition. Instead of purchasing and managing five separate security tools, NordLayer bundles them into one platform with a single admin dashboard. The trade-off is that each individual component is less feature-rich than dedicated enterprise solutions — but for most businesses under 500 employees, the NordLayer implementation is more than sufficient. ## Pricing & Plans NordLayer uses a per-user, per-month pricing model with four tiers. All plans require a **minimum of 5 users** (except Enterprise, which requires 100). Annual billing saves up to 22% compared to monthly payments. ### Current Pricing (March 2026, Annual Billing) | Plan | Price/User/Month | Minimum Users | Min Monthly Cost | Dedicated Server | Key Features | |------|----------------:|:-------------:|----------------:|:----------------:|------| | **Lite** | $8 | 5 | $40 | Not available | VPN access, web protection, download protection, SSO, MFA, Always-On VPN | | **Core** | $11 | 5 | $55 + $40 server | Required (+$40/mo) | All Lite features + DNS filtering, IP allowlisting, device security monitoring, network segmentation | | **Premium** | $14 | 5 | $70 + $40 server | Required (+$40/mo) | All Core features + Cloud Firewall (FWaaS), device posture security, site-to-site connector, Cloud LAN, browser extension | | **Enterprise** | From $7 | 100 | $700+ | Included | Custom-fit solution starting from Lite capabilities, dedicated account manager | ### Hidden Cost: Dedicated Servers The pricing table looks straightforward until you realize that **Core and Premium plans require a dedicated server with a static IP**, which costs an additional **$40 per month**.
Pro Tip: Hidden gateway fees are common across business VPN providers. Our best VPN guide includes a full business VPN cost comparison that shows the true per-user cost once gateway and add-on fees are factored in — so you can compare NordLayer, Perimeter 81, and Twingate on equal footing.
This is not optional on these tiers — it is listed as "required" on the pricing page. For a 10-person team on the Core plan, the real monthly cost is: - 10 users x $11 = $110/month - 1 dedicated server = $40/month - **Total: $150/month ($1,800/year)** This is still competitive compared to enterprise ZTNA solutions (Zscaler Private Access starts at $150+/user/year), but it is significantly more than what the headline "$11/user/month" suggests. ### Payment Methods NordLayer accepts credit card (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover) and bank transfer. No cryptocurrency or alternative payment options — this is a business product with standard invoicing. ### Money-Back Guarantee All plans include a **14-day money-back guarantee**. There is no free trial in the traditional sense, but you can create an account and explore the Control Panel for free before purchasing a subscription. This limited evaluation period is a drawback compared to competitors like Perimeter 81, which previously offered more generous trial terms. ## Apps & Platform Support NordLayer provides native applications across all major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: iOS, Android - **Browser**: Chrome and Firefox extensions (Premium plan only) - **6 devices per license** — each user can connect up to 6 devices simultaneously ### Admin Control Panel The centralized admin dashboard is NordLayer's strongest feature. IT administrators can: - Add, remove, and transfer user accounts - Create teams with different access permissions and gateway assignments - Monitor VPN activity with detailed usage reports - Enforce Always-On VPN policies to prevent employees from disconnecting - Configure device posture requirements (OS version, antivirus, disk encryption) - Set up SSO with identity providers (Azure AD, Okta, OneLogin, JumpCloud, Google Workspace) - Automate user provisioning via SCIM integration - Manage dedicated servers and custom gateways Deployment time is genuinely fast. NordLayer advertises 10 minutes to set up, and our experience confirmed this — from account creation to having team members connected through the VPN took approximately 12 minutes. The onboarding wizard walks administrators through gateway setup, team creation, and user invitations step by step. ### Integration Ecosystem NordLayer integrates with major business tools: - **Identity Providers**: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), Okta, OneLogin, JumpCloud, Google Workspace - **Cloud Platforms**: AWS, Google Cloud, IBM Cloud - **Security Tools**: SentinelOne, Jamf Cloud, CrowdStrike (new partnership in 2026) - **Automation**: SCIM-based automated user provisioning The new **CrowdStrike x NordLayer bundle** (launched March 2026) combines endpoint protection with network security, starting at $2 per device. This partnership positions NordLayer as part of a broader security stack rather than a standalone VPN. ## Speed Performance Speed testing a business VPN is fundamentally different from testing a consumer VPN. NordLayer is not designed to maximize raw download throughput for streaming 4K video — it is designed to provide stable, encrypted connections to business resources with minimal latency impact on productivity applications. ### Server Performance All NordLayer plans support **up to 1 Gbps server performance**. In practice, the actual speed depends on several factors: - **Shared vs. dedicated gateways** — dedicated servers provide more consistent performance since traffic is not shared with other organizations - **Geographic proximity** — connecting to a gateway close to the company's resources minimizes latency - **Number of concurrent users** — shared gateways may slow down during peak usage - **Protocol choice** — NordLynx (WireGuard) delivers the best speeds; OpenVPN and IKEv2 are slower but more compatible ### Real-World Performance In day-to-day business use — accessing cloud applications, file shares, video conferencing, and internal dashboards — NordLayer performed well. We did not experience noticeable lag on video calls or significant delays when accessing cloud-hosted resources through the VPN tunnel. The Always-On VPN feature maintained connections reliably during network transitions on Windows and macOS. The Linux client was a different story. Multiple Gartner Peer Insights reviewers and G2 users have reported **frequent disconnections** on Linux, sometimes requiring manual reconnection multiple times per day. If your organization relies heavily on Linux workstations, this is a serious consideration. ## Streaming & Personal Use Let us be direct: **NordLayer is not for streaming**. It does not unblock Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Amazon Prime Video, or any other geo-restricted platform. It is not designed to, and Nord Security does not market it for this purpose. If you need a VPN for streaming, get [NordVPN](https://nordvpn.com) (consumer product, same parent company) or [Surfshark](https://go.zerotovpn.com/surfshark). NordLayer serves an entirely different market. Similarly, NordLayer does not support torrenting and is not intended for personal privacy browsing. Its purpose is securing business network access — period. ## Comparison: NordLayer vs Business VPN Competitors | Feature | NordLayer | Perimeter 81 | Twingate | Zscaler Private Access | |---------|-----------|-------------|----------|----------------------| | **Starting price** | $8/user/mo | $8/user/mo | Free (up to 5 users) | $150+/user/year | | **Minimum users** | 5 | 5 | None (free tier) | Enterprise only | | **ZTNA** | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | **SASE framework** | Yes | Yes | Partial | Yes (full) | | **ISO 27001** | Yes | Yes | No | Yes | | **SOC 2 Type 2** | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | **HIPAA compliant** | Yes | No | No | Yes | | **SSO integrations** | 5 providers | 5+ providers | 5+ providers | 10+ providers | | **Server locations** | 40+ | 70+ | N/A (connector-based) | 150+ | | **Dedicated servers** | +$40/mo | +$50/mo | N/A | Included | | **24/7 support** | Yes (live chat) | Yes | Business hours | Yes | | **Setup time** | ~10 minutes | ~15 minutes | ~20 minutes | Days to weeks | | **Best for** | SMBs wanting simplicity | Mid-market teams | Developer-focused teams | Large enterprises | NordLayer's main advantage over Perimeter 81 is its HIPAA compliance and the brand trust from Nord Security. Twingate's free tier is attractive for very small teams, but lacks the SASE features that growing organizations need. Zscaler is the enterprise gold standard but costs 3-5x more and requires significantly more setup time. ## What We Do Not Like ### 1. The 5-User Minimum Is Punishing for Small Teams A 3-person startup pays the same as a 5-person team. On the Lite plan, that means $40/month minimum for just 3 actual users — effectively $13.33/user instead of the advertised $8. Twingate's free tier for up to 5 users makes NordLayer look expensive for the smallest teams. ### 2. Dedicated Server Costs Add Up Fast The $40/month dedicated server fee on Core and Premium plans is a significant addition that is easy to overlook when comparing headline prices. For organizations that need multiple gateways in different regions, this cost multiplies quickly. ### 3. Linux Client Reliability Multiple independent reviews and user reports cite stability issues with the Linux client. For organizations with Linux-heavy infrastructure, this is not a minor inconvenience — it is a productivity blocker. ### 4. No Free Trial A 14-day money-back guarantee is not the same as a free trial. You have to commit payment information and go through the purchasing process before you can properly evaluate the platform with your team. Competitors like Twingate offer a genuinely free tier. ### 5. Limited Server Locations With 40+ locations, NordLayer covers major business regions but lacks the global reach of consumer VPNs. For organizations with employees in less common regions (Africa, South America, Central Asia), gateway options may be limited. ### 6. Feature Gating Across Plans Useful features like DNS filtering, IP allowlisting, and the browser extension are locked behind higher-tier plans. The Lite plan is quite bare-bones — you are essentially getting a team VPN with basic threat protection and not much else. ## FAQ **Is NordLayer the same as NordVPN?** No. NordLayer and NordVPN are separate products from the same parent company, Nord Security. NordVPN is a consumer VPN for personal use — streaming, privacy, torrenting. NordLayer is a business network security platform with team management, zero trust features, SSO integration, and centralized administration. They share the same NordLynx protocol and AES-256 encryption, but serve fundamentally different markets. **How much does NordLayer actually cost for a 10-person team?** On the most popular Core plan with annual billing: 10 users at $11/user/month = $110, plus one dedicated server at $40/month = $150/month total ($1,800/year). On the Lite plan without a dedicated server: 10 users at $8/user/month = $80/month ($960/year). Enterprise pricing starts at $7/user/month for 100+ users. **Is NordLayer HIPAA compliant?** Yes. NordLayer's security measures meet the objectives outlined in the HIPAA Security Rules, making it suitable for healthcare organizations handling Protected Health Information (PHI). Combined with ISO 27001 certification and SOC 2 Type 2 compliance, NordLayer has the audit documentation that healthcare compliance officers require. **Can NordLayer replace our existing VPN?** For most SMBs, yes. NordLayer can replace traditional hardware VPN appliances and legacy VPN software with a cloud-native solution that is easier to manage, scales with your team, and provides modern security features like ZTNA and device posture checks. However, organizations with complex multi-site networking requirements may still need NordLayer's Premium plan with site-to-site connectors, or a more robust SD-WAN solution. **Does NordLayer work for remote teams?** This is precisely what NordLayer is designed for. Remote employees install the NordLayer app, authenticate via SSO, and securely access company resources through encrypted VPN tunnels. Administrators can enforce Always-On VPN policies to ensure employees are always connected through the secure gateway. The platform supports Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android. **What identity providers does NordLayer support for SSO?** NordLayer integrates with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD), Okta, OneLogin, JumpCloud, and Google Workspace. SCIM-based automated user provisioning is available for streamlined onboarding and offboarding. SSO is included on all plans, while automated SCIM provisioning is included on Pro and Enterprise plans or available as an add-on on Lite and Core. **Is NordLayer secure enough for financial services?** NordLayer's security credentials — AES-256 encryption, ISO 27001 certification, SOC 2 Type 2 compliance, zero trust architecture, and device posture security — meet the security requirements of most financial services organizations. Multiple Gartner Peer Insights reviewers from the banking industry have rated NordLayer 5 out of 5 stars. However, organizations subject to specific regulatory frameworks should verify compliance requirements with NordLayer's sales team. **How does NordLayer compare to Zscaler or Palo Alto for enterprise use?** NordLayer targets SMBs and mid-market organizations, not large enterprises. Zscaler Private Access and Palo Alto Prisma Access offer more comprehensive SASE capabilities, deeper integrations, and larger global networks — but at significantly higher cost ($150+/user/year) and complexity. NordLayer is the right choice for organizations that need 80% of enterprise SASE functionality at 20% of the cost and complexity. ## Sources
